title: pyrus communis var. dispar
side_effects: Pyrus communis var. dispar can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal issues, including nausea and diarrhea, due to the high concentration of amygdalin, a toxic compound that releases cyanide when ingested. Prolonged consumption of this plant can also lead to cyanide poisoning, which can cause headaches, dizziness, and even respiratory failure in severe cases. In addition, improper use of Pyrus communis var. dispar may cause allergic reactions, including skin irritation and anaphylaxis, particularly in individuals with pre-existing conditions. Furthermore, excessive consumption can disrupt the body's natural potassium levels, potentially leading to muscle weakness and cardiac problems.
